workingDeparture.notes: Assume RI is activated Friday morning before work. Friday evening starts from Hope around 17:00, targets Silver Sands around 19:00, then charges and continues only as far west as is comfortable by about 21:30.

riActivation.preferredTiming: Friday morning before work from Rhode Island
riActivation.fallbackTiming: Only if the morning activation fails; otherwise do not spend Friday evening on RI.
riActivation.notes: Current plan assumes RI is complete before the 17:00 Friday road departure from Hope.

parkStopPlanning.meaning: For broad planning, count two hours per non-overnight park stop to cover leaving the highway, setup, activation, teardown, and returning to the route.

roveCounting.multiParkRule: Multi-entity activations count once per unique POTA entity; a 2-fer counts as 2 entities.
roveCounting.eveningStrategy: Evening activations can intentionally count toward the current UTC rove day and reduce the next local day's required load.

vehicle.chargingNetworkRule: Prefer Tesla Superchargers only for planned charging stops; do not treat generic EV chargers as equivalent when judging feasibility.
vehicle.notes: Account for roughly 30 minutes at Tesla Superchargers after each 2.5 hours of driving. Friday likely needs a charge after Silver Sands, with Milford, Fairfield, Darien, Stamford, Greenwich, Dobbs Ferry, and Tarrytown all appearing along the westbound line.

weekendPlanning.notes: Saturday and Sunday have no work window, so they can carry more driving and more activations than weekdays.

candidateHighRoveDay.status: current working plan: make DC the large rove day because of the dense n-fer opportunities

constraints.jurisdictionActivationGoal: Activate every state and Washington, DC, actually crossed on the selected route. Pennsylvania is optional: it is only required if the route deliberately goes through the Philadelphia / John Heinz branch.
constraints.multiParkActivationPriority: Nice to have; do not optimize the route around 2/3/4-fers unless they are convenient.

dcActivation.timing: Handle DC on Sunday June 21 from the Foggy Bottom hotel, with an optional friend meetup if timing works.
dcActivation.notes: The friend activation is nice to have, not a route constraint. Saturday night is already in DC, so Sunday should use the local density and then continue south to the Fredericksburg hotel.

outreach.purpose: Contact common activators near short-listed corridor parks for practical access, parking, antenna, and operating vibe checks.
outreach.emailDraft: Hi <name>, I am planning a June 2026 POTA road trip from Rhode Island to Florida and noticed you have activated <park/reference>. I am considering it for a brief activation while passing through and would appreciate any practical advice: easy parking, good operating spots, antenna constraints, safety/access issues, and whether it is realistic for about an hour on site. Thanks and 73, Rob / N1RWJ

timelineDrafts.name: Fixed-anchor southbound plan
timelineDrafts.recommendation: Use Friday to bank RI and CT, charge after Silver Sands, then stop in the lower Hudson / southwest Connecticut corridor by about 21:30. Saturday covers NY, NJ, DE, and MD before the Foggy Bottom hotel, with PA as an optional Philadelphia branch, with MD allowed to slide after 20:00 EDT if needed for Sunday UTC credit.
assumptions: RI is activated Friday morning before work.
Friday road departure is from Hope, RI around 17:00 local.
Hope to Silver Sands is about 1h47 by Google Maps at the Friday departure time, so a roughly 19:00 CT arrival is realistic.
After the Silver Sands activation, charge the Tesla and keep driving only until roughly 21:30 local.
Friday does not need to cover NY; Saturday can start with a lower-Hudson NY activation.

Saturday must cover NY, NJ, DE, and MD before the Foggy Bottom hotel. PA is only required if the selected route goes through Pennsylvania; if MD happens after 20:00 EDT, it counts toward the Sunday UTC day.
Sunday remains the large DC / NoVA rove day, ending at the booked Fredericksburg hotel.

days.area: Hope, RI to Silver Sands, then lower Hudson / southwest Connecticut
days.focus: RI in the morning, CT at Silver Sands after work, Tesla charge, then a modest westbound hotel by about 21:30.
days.notes: Tarrytown/Nyack is ideal if the post-charge drive feels fine; Stamford/White Plains are acceptable if fatigue or charging timing pushes back.

days.area: Lower Hudson / southwest Connecticut to Foggy Bottom
days.focus: Activate NY, NJ, DE, and MD on the way to the fixed DC hotel; add PA only if choosing the Philadelphia / John Heinz branch.
days.notes: Default route candidate: Tallman for NY, Cheesequake for NJ, Fox Point for DE, Fort McHenry for MD, then Foggy Bottom. Optional PA branch inserts US-0512 John Heinz NWR between Cheesequake and Fox Point.

days.focus: Make this the large rove day using the DC density, then continue south to the booked Fredericksburg hotel.
days.notes: LBJ Grove 7-fer is the top verification item; Mason Neck and Prince William Forest are southbound add-ons if energy and timing allow.

days.focus: Workday southbound push to the booked Florence stop while covering NC/SC as needed.
days.notes: Because Monday has a 13:00-17:00 work block, park choices need to be low-detour and close to the I-95 line.

days.focus: Use Tuesday to reduce the southern schedule risk and decide where the GA activation and hotel positioning should land.
days.notes: Exact overnight remains open; choose it based on charging, hotel availability, and how much distance remains to Ocala.

days.focus: Continue south; reaching Ocala Wednesday is acceptable but not required.
days.notes: If Florida is not banked earlier, pick a reliable low-detour Florida activation before or near Ocala.

days.focus: Stay at mother's house in Ocala and protect the final arrival day.
days.notes: This can be the low-stress Florida buffer night. If already in Ocala Wednesday, Thursday becomes flexible.

days.area: Ocala to Singer Island / Riviera Beach
days.focus: Final arrival for Field Day weekend.
days.notes: Florida should ideally be complete before this day so arrival is not dependent on another park stop.

areaBudgets.notes: Working Friday 17:00-18:00 departure block. If RI is activated Friday morning, this can be a simple CT reposition or CT activation near the first overnight; otherwise add about 1 hour for an outbound RI activation.
High-density corridor where multi-entity stops and evening UTC-day strategy could help a 10-entity rove goal.
Likely weekend work. Needs traffic-aware park choices and charging paired with food or a nearby activation.
Second weekend positioning block. If Sunday DC works, handle the friend activation here and keep the rest of the week southbound.
Best handled as a buffer/workday block around the DC evening activation rather than a high-mileage day.
A tight weekday segment because it must fit around the 13:00-17:00 work window unless the evening runs late.
Likely the hardest southern block. SC and GA should use very low-detour parks or multi-entity opportunities if available.
Useful arrival-buffer segment if Thursday gets long; can also carry the first Florida activation.
Keep the final Florida activation close to the route or destination unless earlier days create real slack.

planningConclusions: The large rove day should remain Sunday June 21 in DC / NoVA, because the DC n-fer density is much stronger than the Friday/Saturday corridor.
Friday should assume RI is already complete, then run Hope -> Silver Sands -> Tesla charge -> lower Hudson / southwest Connecticut hotel, stopping by about 21:30.
Do not push to Meadowlands Friday if it makes the night too late; Tarrytown/Nyack is enough westward progress and avoids a Saturday NJ-to-NY double-back.
If Friday stops in Stamford or White Plains instead, Saturday remains possible but has about 20 extra minutes of mapped driving versus Tarrytown/Nyack.
Saturday must cover NY, NJ, DE, and MD before DC. PA is optional; it only becomes required if the selected route goes through the Philadelphia / US-0512 John Heinz branch.
The PA optional branch adds only about 10 minutes of mapped driving versus the no-PA chain, but it adds another physical activation stop. Use it only if the extra entity is worth the stop time.
John Heinz NWR is the practical PA routing candidate near I-95, but its exact POTA reference must be verified before treating it as final.
If Saturday runs late, let Fort McHenry / MD happen after 20:00 EDT; that banks MD for the Sunday UTC day while still keeping the southbound route coherent.

firstWeekendPlan.utcNote: In June EDT, the UTC day changes at 20:00 local. Friday evening activations after 20:00 local count toward the next UTC day.

fridayNightAnchors.name: Tarrytown / Nyack, NY
fridayNightAnchors.role: preferred Friday hotel area if Silver Sands and charging stay tight enough for a roughly 21:30 stop
pros: Gets into NY without requiring a Friday night NY activation
Starts Saturday close to Tallman Mountain State Park
Avoids driving to New Jersey and then backtracking for NY
cons: Requires about 59 minutes of driving after the Milford Supercharger
Can push too late if Silver Sands, charging, or dinner runs long
fridayNightAnchors.saturdayImpact: Best current balance for Saturday: Tallman -> Cheesequake -> John Heinz (US-0512) -> Fox Point -> Fort McHenry -> Foggy Bottom.

fridayNightAnchors.name: Stamford / White Plains
fridayNightAnchors.role: fatigue-aware fallback if the Friday night westward push needs to end earlier
pros: Keeps Friday closer to the 21:30 comfort limit
Still starts Saturday close enough to Tallman
Preserves a coherent Saturday route without forcing Meadowlands
cons: Adds about 20 minutes to Saturday versus Tarrytown/Nyack
Leaves a little more lower-Hudson movement for Saturday morning
fridayNightAnchors.saturdayImpact: Still viable; the Saturday chain grows to roughly 5h55m of mapped driving before stops.

fridayNightAnchors.name: Milford / Fairfield, CT
fridayNightAnchors.role: safety fallback if the Silver Sands activation or charging consumes the evening
cons: Leaves NY, NJ, DE, MD, and DC for Saturday, plus the optional PA branch if selected
Likely requires an earlier Saturday start
fridayNightAnchors.saturdayImpact: Use only if Friday timing or fatigue makes the westward hotel options unattractive.

saturdayRove.strategy: Saturday June 20 defaults to the no-PA southbound chain: start with NY near the lower Hudson, then activate NJ, DE, and MD before the fixed Foggy Bottom hotel. PA is an optional Philadelphia / US-0512 John Heinz branch if the extra entity is worth another physical stop. MD can happen after the 20:00 EDT UTC rollover if the day runs late.

timingAssumptions.source: Google Maps MCP route checks from Hope to Silver Sands, Friday post-activation hotel anchors, and Saturday lower-Hudson-to-DC chains; park hours and exact POTA references still need confirmation.
timingAssumptions.fridayActivationTiming: RI is assumed complete Friday morning. Silver Sands around 19:00 should be workable, with the official after-hours fishing parking as a useful access detail if the operation reaches the sunset boundary.
timingAssumptions.saturdayEveningTiming: If Fort McHenry / MD happens after 20:00 EDT Saturday, it counts toward the Sunday UTC rove day and reduces Sunday pressure.
timingAssumptions.chargingStopsIncluded: Friday likely needs a 30-minute Tesla Supercharger stop after Silver Sands. Saturday likely needs one or two additional 30-minute Supercharger stops depending on starting charge and hotel charging.
timingAssumptions.accessNotes: Silver Sands is listed as 08:00-sunset, and CT Parks notes after-hours fishing parking. Tallman is dawn-to-dusk, so it is a Saturday morning NY stop, not a Friday night solution. If the optional PA branch is selected, John Heinz NWR trails are sunrise-to-sundown and need to be a daytime stop.

fridayEveningOptions.name: Recommended Friday: Hope -> Silver Sands -> Milford charge -> Tarrytown/Nyack hotel
fridayEveningOptions.route: Hope -> Silver Sands -> Tesla Supercharger near Milford/Fairfield -> Tarrytown or Nyack
fridayEveningOptions.estimatedTotalLabel: about 5h from a 17:00 departure, including a 30-minute charge but excluding dinner variation
fridayEveningOptions.comfort: best if the post-charge drive still feels good
fridayEveningOptions.notes: Hope to Silver Sands is about 1h47. Silver Sands to Milford Supercharger to Tarrytown is about 1h07; to Nyack is about 1h11. This fits a 21:30-ish stop only if the activation and charge stay tight.

fridayEveningOptions.name: Fallback Friday: Hope -> Silver Sands -> charge -> Stamford/White Plains hotel
fridayEveningOptions.route: Hope -> Silver Sands -> Tesla Supercharger near Milford/Fairfield/Darien -> Stamford or White Plains
fridayEveningOptions.estimatedTotalLabel: about 4h45-5h from a 17:00 departure depending on charging location
fridayEveningOptions.notes: Stamford is about 46 minutes from Silver Sands via the Milford charger; White Plains is about 60 minutes. This preserves Saturday feasibility without forcing a too-late Friday.

saturdayRove.restOfTripComfortRule: Saturday should protect the Foggy Bottom hotel arrival and Sunday DC rove. Default to the no-PA route unless the extra US-0512 stop is clearly worth the time.

saturdayOvernight.reason: Saturday is now primarily a positioning day to the fixed DC-area hotel, not a maximize-count rove day.

openQuestions: Decide whether to avoid PA by using the NJ Turnpike / Delaware Memorial Bridge line, or deliberately add the US-0512 John Heinz branch for another entity.
Pick the Friday hotel area after checking availability: Tarrytown/Nyack preferred, Stamford/White Plains fallback, Milford/Fairfield only as a safety fallback.
Confirm exact Fort McHenry operating position and whether it reliably counts US-0928 plus the relevant trail references.
Identify Saturday Tesla Supercharger pairings around central NJ / Philadelphia and Delaware / Baltimore.
Find a legal Saturday after-20:00 DC-area activation close to Foggy Bottom only if Saturday arrives early enough.

sundayDcRove.strategy: Use Sunday June 21 for the true dense rove from the Foggy Bottom hotel. Start with the LBJ Memorial Grove overlap in DC, add Dyke Marsh if the imported 7-fer evidence verifies, then continue south through Mason Neck and Prince William before the fixed Fredericksburg hotel night.

Low-detour Virginia add-on before the Fredericksburg hotel.

sunday.routingNote: Foggy Bottom to LBJ Grove should be a short local hop; the nearby parking-lot/picnic-table option is described as a 4-fer if the exact 7-fer walk-out spot is not practical.

sunday.routingNote: Rock Creek Park to Mason Neck State Park is roughly 55-63 minutes depending on the exact route.

assumptions: Friday road departure is from Hope, RI around 17:00 after RI has been activated in the morning.
All overnights are now hotels or family/home stays; camping is no longer part of the plan.
Saturday night is fixed at a hotel in the Foggy Bottom area of Washington, DC.
Sunday night is fixed at a hotel in Fredericksburg, VA.
Monday June 22 is fixed in Florence, SC.
Thursday night is planned at mother's house in Ocala, FL; arriving Wednesday is acceptable but not required.
Broad planning uses 2 hours per non-overnight park stop, including detour, setup, activation, teardown, and return to the highway.
Weekdays still have a 13:00-17:00 work block unless explicitly changed.
